justification: All 7 ACs implemented and mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. Collect-all-errors pattern explicitly used (no fail-fast). AC5 conditional required for express only, AC6 conditional forbidden for standard only — distinct and correct. Error shape is { field, code } throughout. AC7 tested with both a 2-error and 3-error multi-failure request. 15 tests cover all 7 ACs including boundary conditions.

observations.text: AC5/AC6 distinction is precise: saturday_delivery=false is valid for express (AC5) but triggers error for standard (AC6), showing correct conditional logic
AC7 covered with two multi-error tests (2-error and 3-error combos) with exact count assertions
Boundary case weight_kg=1000 explicitly tested as valid (strict > comparison)
No external validation library used
Assumptions that undefined weight_kg and undefined shipment_type are valid may be questionable, but spec ambiguity makes this a reasonable interpretation rather than a defect

trials.justification: All 7 ACs implemented and mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. Collect-all-errors pattern confirmed (independent if-blocks pushing to shared array). AC5 conditional correct (express + undefined saturday_delivery → REQUIRED_FOR_EXPRESS, false accepted). AC6 distinct and correct (standard + any defined saturday_delivery → NOT_APPLICABLE_FOR_STANDARD). Error shape is { field, code } (ValidationError type). AC7 tested with both 2-error and 3-error multi-failure requests. 15 tests cover all 7 ACs. No external libraries.

observations.text: AC5 and AC6 are clearly distinct with correct conditional logic — saturday_delivery=false accepted for express, rejected for standard
AC7 tested with two separate multi-error scenarios (2-error and 3-error), exceeding minimum requirements
Boundary case at weight_kg=1000 explicitly tested as valid
Whitespace-only recipient_name handled via trim(), going beyond basic missing/empty checks
weight_kg=undefined not guarded — assumption noted but could silently skip validation for missing weight field

trials.justification: The agent describes an implementation that covers all 7 ACs: collect-all-errors pattern (AC7), conditional required saturday_delivery for express (AC5), conditional forbidden saturday_delivery for standard (AC6), and error objects with {field, code} shape. Tests cover all 7 ACs including multi-error scenarios for AC7. However, the agent only presented a plan and asked for approval rather than showing the actual executed code and passing test output, so confidence is moderate — the described design is correct but execution was not demonstrated.
